target: data/sections.json:part4.html Section 4.6 — Row 2 of the gap-audit-table AND the TLDR sentence that references "(10 / 9 / 1)"
what_changed_since_iss_filing: ISS-1132 was filed 2026-04-18 capturing three layers (ai_manifest=10, visible wins.html grid=9, claim_index.json strict=1).

As of 2026-05-16 verification: ai_manifest.json=10 ✓ (unchanged), wins.html "Prospective Wins" section header now reads "(10)" — closing the originally-flagged 10-vs-9 wins-page gap. The author appears to have reconciled the wins.html visible count to match the manifest at some point between April 18 and May 16. Cannot date precisely without commit-history bisect.

A separate divergence emerged: predictions.html banner now reads "PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12 predictions confirmed before data came in". This is a 12-vs-10 gap with the manifest/wins.html consensus, and a 12-vs-1 gap with the strict API claim_index.json. The pre-existing poller note (2026-05-15) records: wins=10, predictions=12, April-5 baseline=11.

claim_index.json strict_prospective_confirmed remains 1 (only pred-eclipse-tier3 carries claim_class==prospective_confirmed in api/current/claim_index.json — verified at api/current/claim_index.json this run).

live.html footer: row 4 of the same table claims live.html says "67 confirmed". As of today live.html footer shows "69 confirmed" (line 772, footer-timestamps span). Row 4 is therefore now factually stale; that is NOT in the scope of THIS EXP (ISS-1132 is the prospective metric specifically) but is flagged below for a separate ISS.

evidence_collected_this_run.predictions.html banner: PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12 predictions confirmed before data came in
evidence_collected_this_run.live.html footer (out-of-scope observati: 69 confirmed (was 67 in our review; row 4 of Section 4.6 table now stale)

kernel_of_truth: The dome author has been substantively transparent in methodology.json and registry_semantics about this exact gap. methodology.json: "Verify the pre-data wording, then verify the cited outcome independently." ai_manifest.json registry_semantics: "Do not treat the display ratio as a substitute for claim-by-claim prospective audit." Under those rules, the three counts ARE consistent — each is correct under its definition: ai_manifest/wins.html count items in the wins.html grid carried as prospective (10), predictions.html banner counts items on predictions.html that the author considers "predicted before data came in" (12 — a broader population including ones not promoted to wins.html), and claim_index.json applies the strict audit definition (1 — only pred-eclipse-tier3 has both pre-data wording verified and outcome confirmable). The author publicly says these are different things. So under his own rules the counts are not "wrong" — they are measuring different things. The critique is narrower (and is the one Section 4.6 already makes): the disclaimer about display-counts lives in machine-readable files; the visible banners do not carry the same disclaimer. A reader who lands on predictions.html sees "PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12" with no caveat.

patches.find: most sharply, a three-layer disagreement on the prospective_confirmed sub-count (10 / 9 / 1) — each publication of the same aggregate gives a different number.
patches.replace: most sharply, a three-layer disagreement on the prospective_confirmed sub-count (10 / 12 / 1 as of 2026-05-16; was 10 / 9 / 1 when we first audited) — each publication of the same aggregate gives a different number, and the gap shifts as the author edits one surface without propagating to the others.

patches.find: prospective_confirmed = 10 in ai_manifest.jsonVisible grid on homepage shows 9 prospective-confirmed items; claim_index.json strict subset counts 1Three different publications of the same aggregate disagree: 10 / 9 / 1. Whichever is correct, at least two are wrong. (Newly-discovered; not previously tracked in our issue queue.)
patches.replace: prospective_confirmed = 10 in ai_manifest.json (and overview.json's prospective_display, and wins.html's "Prospective Wins" section header)predictions.html banner: "PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12 predictions confirmed before data came in"; claim_index.json strict subset (claim_class==prospective_confirmed) counts 1 (only pred-eclipse-tier3)Three different publications of the same aggregate disagree: 10 / 12 / 1 as of 2026-05-16. The original 10-vs-9 manifest-vs-wins-grid gap we flagged in our 2026-04-18 audit has closed (wins.html "Prospective Wins (10)" now matches the manifest), but the 12-vs-10 predictions.html-banner-vs-wins-page gap persists, and the strict API definition still yields 1. The reader who lands on predictions.html sees 12 with no caveat; the reader who lands on wins.html sees 10; the reader who consumes claim_index.json sees 1. The dome's own methodology.json and ai_manifest.json:registry_semantics tell machine readers "display counts are bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit" — but that caveat does not appear next to the predictions.html banner where it would be load-bearing. (First flagged 2026-04-18; partially-self-healed and re-emerged in different shape; verified 2026-05-16.)

holes_addressed: ISS-1132: re-grep all three layers (done — actually four+ now: ai_manifest, overview, wins.html, predictions.html, claim_index.json).

ISS-1132: decide canonical citation (the three numbers 10/12/1 are each correct under their own definition; the canonical cite is the comparison itself, and the visible-layer caveat-absence is the critique).
ISS-1132: propagate (patch updates the existing Section 4.6 row 2 and TLDR; no propagation to other files needed because Section 4.6 is the canonical hub for this critique).

anticipated_objections.objection: The three numbers are not really "disagreement" — they measure different populations. ai_manifest counts wins.html grid items; predictions.html counts predictions.html items; claim_index applies a strict definition. The dome author publishes those definitions. Crying "disagreement" is a strawman.
anticipated_objections.response: Accepted — we have updated the kernel-of-truth and the row text to acknowledge each count is correct under its own definition. The remaining critique (which Section 4.6 has carried since first authoring) is that the disclaimers live in machine-readable files while the visible banners do not carry the same disclaimer. The "PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED: 12" banner on predictions.html is not next to the methodology.json caveat. That is the load-bearing claim we keep.

anticipated_objections.objection: You should be calling this a "closing" — the 10-vs-9 gap that you originally flagged in ISS-1132 has resolved. The dome author edited wins.html section header from 9 to 10 to match the manifest. That is a rigor improvement and you should credit it without immediately replacing the closed gap with a new one.
anticipated_objections.response: Accepted partially — the patch text now explicitly says "The original 10-vs-9 manifest-vs-wins-grid gap we flagged in our 2026-04-18 audit has closed." We credit the close. But the predictions.html-banner-vs-everything gap is independently observable today, was flagged by our poller, and is the same structural pattern (multiple surfaces, same wording "prospective", different counts, no visible-layer caveat). Combining "credit the close + note the new gap" is the honest read; pretending the new gap doesn't exist would be the dishonest one.

anticipated_objections.objection: predictions.html=12 may include items that are NOT in wins.html by design — e.g. PRED-XXX entries that are still pending observation. Calling that "disagreement" with wins.html=10 is a category error.
anticipated_objections.response: Conceded as a possibility. We do not have a per-PRED audit cross-reference to confirm which 2 items in predictions.html's "12" are not in wins.html's "10". Even granting that — the user-facing read remains: predictions.html banner displays "12 PROSPECTIVE CONFIRMED" in a load-bearing position, with no caveat that this is a different population from wins.html's 10. The reader does not have the per-PRED reconciliation in hand. The critique survives.

If two of those six rows (the 10-vs-9 prospective gap and the 67-vs-69 confirmed-footer gap) have partially or fully closed, the headline number "six" may need to be revised to "six initially flagged; two partially/fully closed and two new gaps emerged" or simply re-counted. Not done in this EXP.
summary_for_decider: EXP-396 patches Section 4.6 (Registry Framing Audit) Row 2 and its TLDR to reflect today's empirical state of the prospective_confirmed three-layer count: 10 / 12 / 1 (was 10 / 9 / 1 at original audit). The 10-vs-9 wins-page-vs-manifest gap that ISS-1132 originally flagged has closed (we credit it explicitly). A new predictions.html-banner-vs-wins-page gap (12 vs 10) emerged and is now the dominant divergence. Strict API claim_index.json still yields 1. The kernel-of-truth is preserved: under the author's own definitions, each count is correct against its own population; the critique is the visible-layer disclaimer-absence relative to the machine-readable layer. review_class=deep-attack because this revises a load-bearing argument with fresh empirical inputs and acknowledges a partial self-heal by the dome author — not pure refinement. OUT-OF-SCOPE FOLLOW-UP: Row 4 of the same table is now factually stale (live.html footer reads "69 confirmed" not "67"). Recommend new ISS to update or remove Row 4.
newsworthy: Dome partially self-healed the prospective-count discrepancy our review flagged; immediately re-opened a wider one on a different page
